title: Add restricted project-understanding assistant with versioned evidence records
notes: Created from human suggestion #24 (submitted by mannyrayner on 2026-05-30) and broadened after follow-up discussion. The requested capability is an admin/trusted-user project-understanding assistant that answers high-level questions about publicly available aspects of C-LARA-2 using repository materials as evidence. Initial implementation work began on 2026-05-31 with a core prompt-wrapper/request function and Markdown evidence-record rendering helpers. By 2026-06-01 the architecture had been revised to delegate repository exploration to `codex exec` rather than a normal API/retrieval wrapper, and a first admin-only platform implementation was working: `src/core/project_understanding.py` builds the versioned prompt, invokes `codex exec` in read-only/non-interactive mode, passes `OPENAI_API_KEY` through a reduced environment, resolves common Windows npm Codex shims, captures elapsed time/stdout/stderr/exit status/model/prompt version/token count where extractable from stdout or stderr, computes and labels a conservative output-priced upper-bound cost estimate when only total Codex tokens are available (with local OpenAI Usage comparisons currently suggesting the estimate can be at least three times higher than actual API charges), parses the final answer, and can render/write versionable records. The Django platform now exposes `/admin-tools/project-understanding/` for staff users, queues runs through Django Q, records `TaskUpdate` progress and heartbeat messages, persists request/result JSON under `MEDIA_ROOT/admin_project_understanding/`, and polls a status endpoint until the answer is available. Manual smoke tests produced plausible cited answers for repository-summary and annotated-text-format questions, demonstrating that Codex can inspect repository files itself. Remaining work includes an explicit export/review flow into `docs/project_understanding/`, reviewer assessment controls, citation/path sanitization for non-admin contexts, exact-cost reconciliation if Codex exposes input/cached-input/output usage data, hard budget/rate limiting, curated evaluation records for the report, and maintaining the now-resolved projects migration graph: the pre-existing `0036_profile_byok_fields` versus `0038_projectimagestyle_disallow_text_in_images` branch was merged in `0039`, and `0040` captures the remaining detected model-state changes so fresh-database admin-tool test runs are clean again. Priority remains P2: strategically important for project maintenance and the C-LARA-2 autonomy/authorship evidence case, but restricted until review, cost, prompt-injection, and evidence-quality controls are complete.
